pages.text: Serums are not brewed from nothing. They are built up a ladder of $(flesh)compounds$() — increasingly refined chemical bases, each made in the $(l:3_machines/bio_lab)Bio-Lab$() from the one below.
pages.title: The Compound Ladder

pages.text: $(bold)The ladder:$()$(br)$(li)$(flesh)Organic Compound$() — the base; refined from common organic matter.$(li)$(flesh)Unstable Compound$() — volatile; also the route to gunpowder.$(li)$(flesh)Exotic Compound$() — the reactant behind the stronger serums.$(li)$(flesh)Genetic Compound$() — carries genetic information; the basis of the breeding and growth serums.

$(bold)Additives.$() Alongside the compounds the Lab makes $(flesh)Healing Additive$() and $(flesh)Decaying Additive$() — modifiers folded into a brew to push a serum toward restoration or toward decay. The recipe viewer shows which serum wants which.
pages.title: Additives
